metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default for the pinned baseline in AC4 — e.g. 'stored as `tests/unit/fixtures/criteria_routing_baseline.json`, committed in the same PR as a pre-change capture, and asserted by `jq` diff in the test' — so the executor has a concrete, safe implementation path.
findings.finding: AC4 requires a test that diffs `criteria_routing.json` against 'a pinned baseline snapshot of the untouched criteria', but the plan does not specify where this baseline lives (file path, inline fixture, or generated artifact), leaving the executor to make an unguided implementation choice that could produce a fixture that doesn't actually pin the pre-change state.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4: 'The baseline fixture is `tests/unit/fixtures/criteria_routing_baseline.json`, committed to the repo before the JSON edit (e.g. by copying the current `criteria_routing.json` in the first commit of the PR). The test asserts `jq --sort-keys . baseline.json == jq --sort-keys . routing.json` only for criteria outside the approved set.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'pinned baseline snapshot' referenced in AC4: specify its format (inline dict, separate fixture file, etc.), its storage location, the exact set of criteria it covers (the untouched set), and how it is initially created — co-locate this with AC4 or the test file spec.
findings.finding: AC4's diff-test references a 'pinned baseline snapshot of the untouched criteria' as the oracle, but neither the snapshot's format, its storage location, nor the procedure for creating/pinning it are defined anywhere in the plan — an executor implementing this test has no specification to work from.
findings.suggested_fix: In AC4, replace 'a pinned baseline snapshot' with a concrete specification, e.g.: 'a hardcoded dict in the test asserting the exact `block_threshold` and `default_posture` values for each of the N criteria NOT in the approved set {COH,E2,F1,G5,G6,T5e,T4,G1G2,T1,T8,E4}, read from the live `criteria_routing.json` and compared against those pinned values.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in AC4 where the 'pinned baseline snapshot' lives (e.g., `tests/fixtures/criteria_routing_baseline.json`), what it contains (full routing or only the untouched criteria), and that it must be created as part of this work.
findings.finding: AC4 requires 'a test that diffs the packaged routing against a pinned baseline snapshot of the untouched criteria', but no baseline snapshot file exists anywhere in the repository — this is an unverifiable pre-condition assertion for the test.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: 'a test that diffs the packaged routing against a pinned baseline snapshot of the untouched criteria'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Change 'Plan-review unit tests **updated**' to 'Plan-review unit tests **created**' (or 'written') in AC2, since no per-criterion threshold/posture tests currently exist for any of the 11 criteria in scope.
findings.finding: AC2 says 'Plan-review unit tests updated and green', implying there are pre-existing tests that assert per-criterion `block_threshold` or `default_posture` for the 11 named criteria. A repo-wide grep finds no such tests — there is nothing to 'update'; these tests must be newly created.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'Plan-review unit tests updated and green'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the pinned baseline snapshot file (name, location, and format) as an explicit edit target in the Scope section, and clarify that it must be created as part of this plan.
findings.finding: The 'pinned baseline snapshot' that AC4's diff-based test relies on has no pre-existing file in the repo. AC4 says 'a test that diffs the packaged routing against a pinned baseline snapshot of the untouched criteria' but no such snapshot file exists anywhere in the codebase.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC4: '…asserted by a test that diffs the packaged routing against a pinned baseline snapshot of the untouched criteria.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in AC2 that `tests/unit/test_threshold_recalibration.py` is a **new** file to be created (not an existing file to be updated), and confirm it is an explicit edit target.
findings.finding: The test file `tests/unit/test_threshold_recalibration.py` named in AC2's proving command does not exist in the repository — it must be created from scratch, not 'updated'. The plan's language ('Plan-review unit tests updated') implies pre-existing tests to modify, which misrepresents the edit as a modification when it is a net-new creation.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC2: 'Plan-review unit tests updated and green; … `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_threshold_recalibration.py -v`'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'Plan-review unit tests updated' to 'A new test file `tests/unit/test_threshold_recalibration.py` is created' in AC2.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand or supplement the AC2 proving command to exercise the full plan-review unit test suite (e.g. `pytest tests/unit/test_threshold_recalibration.py tests/unit/test_plan_review/ -v`), not just the single new file, so 'updated and green' is fully verified.
findings.finding: AC2 claims 'Plan-review unit tests updated and green' (implying the full plan-review unit test suite passes), but the only stated proving command runs a single file (`test_threshold_recalibration.py`), leaving other plan-review unit tests unexercised by the documented proof step.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2 / Proving command line
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace the proving command in AC2 with one that covers all plan-review unit tests, e.g.:
`.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/ -k 'plan_review or threshold' -v`
or a dedicated make target `make test-plan-review` that runs the full suite.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the CI parity gates (`.github/workflows/test.yml`, `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml`) as known consumers of `criteria_routing.json` and confirm they pass unchanged after the threshold/posture edits.
findings.finding: The CI parity gates in `.github/workflows/test.yml` and `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` both read `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json` and run parity checks against it. These are consumers outside `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/` that are not enumerated in the plan's scope or acknowledged as requiring a re-run/validation after the JSON changes.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Edit only the routing JSON + the plan-review unit tests'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md section capturing: (a) the adjudication data behind each threshold choice, (b) why E1/T3/A1 were NOT promoted, and (c) the rollout coupling dependency on the permissive-rollout child.
findings.finding: The plan describes a deliberate BREAKING behavioral change to the plan-review routing system but does not include an ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc entry capturing the decision rationale, the data behind the threshold choices, and the rollout coupling strategy.
findings.location: Scope section / Acceptance Criteria — no documentation artifact mentioned
findings.suggested_fix: Create `docs/adr/NNNN-plan-review-threshold-recalibration.md` with sections: Context (adjudication results), Decision (per-criterion table), Consequences (breaking change, rollout coupling, rollback via single-commit JSON revert), and Not-decided (E1/T3 deferred rationale).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the staged-rollout mechanism for this commit: either add an explicit prerequisite (child plan must be merged first and state what 'first' means operationally), or note that the internal-only + trivial-revert path is accepted as sufficient staging for this tool's risk level.
findings.finding: The staged rollout mechanism for this behavioral change is underspecified: the plan says rollout is 'coupled to the permissive-rollout child (ship the wider set first)' but does not state whether that ordering is enforced, whether a feature flag gates the threshold tightening, or what the coexistence window looks like. A reader cannot determine whether this is a hard prerequisite or advisory guidance.
findings.location: ## Scope — 'Rollout is coupled to the permissive-rollout child (ship the wider set first, tune via the calibration harness)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence under Scope: 'This commit MUST NOT be merged until raptorial-galloping-dragon (the permissive-rollout child) is deployed; that child is the staged ramp. Once live-corpus block-rate delta is acceptable, this commit lands as a single atomic threshold update.' If no flag is warranted (internal tool, trivial revert), explicitly state that acceptance.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in AC2 that `tests/unit/test_threshold_recalibration.py` is a **new file** to be created (not an existing file to update), and enumerate the test cases it must contain so the proving command can actually be run.
findings.finding: The AC proving command names `tests/unit/test_threshold_recalibration.py` but that file does not exist anywhere in the repository — it is an unverifiable assertion that a testable artifact exists, and the AC is gated on it.
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite AC2 as: 'Create `tests/unit/test_threshold_recalibration.py` containing [the six lowered-threshold assertions + four promoted-posture assertions + T4-unchanged assertion + baseline-diff assertion]. Proving command: `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_threshold_recalibration.py -v`'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
